results.title: Software Engineering Senior Advisor - Evernorth | CareerSync
highlights:
# Software Engineering Senior Advisor - Evernorth
[...]
The job profile for this position is Software Engineering Senior Advisor, which is a Band 4 Senior Contributor Career Track Role.
[...]
- About the role
- The Cigna Group is a global health service company that uses technology and data to improve how people access and experience care. The Software Engineering Senior Advisor is a hands-on technical leader who designs and steers AI, machine learning operations (MLOps), and data engineering solutions at scale, while partnering closely with business, product, and technology stakeholders to align delivery with strategy, risk, and value.
[...]
- AI & machine learning engineering
- Design and review end-to-end AI/ML solutions: model lifecycle, feature patterns, serving patterns, and integration with business applications.
[...]
Partner with data science and product teams to turn prototypes into production systems with clear ownership, monitoring, and maintenance paths.
[...]
Stay current on responsible AI considerations relevant to healthcare and regulated data (e.g., explain ability, bias awareness, data governance handoffs).
[...]
- MLOps
[...]
- Define and implement MLOps practices: CI/CD for models, registries, deployment strategies, A/B or shadow deployments where appropriate, and rollback.
[...]
Ensure observability for models and pipelines: performance drift, data quality, latency, and business-aligned KPIs.
[...]
- Data Engineering
- Lead design of reliable, scalable data pipelines and platforms that feed training, batch scoring, and real-time inference.
[...]
Promote data quality, lineage, and contract thinking so ML and analytics consumers trust what they build on.
[...]
- Qualifications
- Education & experience
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience).
[...]
Typically 8+ years of software/data engineering experience, including substantial work in at least two of: ML/AI systems, MLOps, or data engineering at scale.
[...]
- Technical skills
- Strong proficiency in Python and/or other languages common in data/ML stacks (e.g., Scala, Java) as used in the target environment.
[...]
Cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and experience with containerization, orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes, Airflow, similar), and CI/CD.
[...]
Data: SQL, batch/streaming patterns, and familiarity with data lakes/warehouses and ELT/ETL.
[...]
ML lifecycle: model packaging, serving (batch and/or online), experiment tracking, and production monitoring.
[...]
Hands-on with LLM/GenAI patterns, guardrails, and evaluation in production settings (as applicable to the team’s strategy).

# [Hiring] VP Of InQbator Data Science @Cigna Healthcare
[...]
1wk ago - Cigna Healthcare is hiring a remote VP Of InQbator Data Science. 💸 Salary: usd 178,500 - 297,500 per year 📍Location: USA
[...]
The VP of InQbator Data Science will drive healthcare innovation by enhancing the accuracy, speed, and efficiency of automated, evidence-based clinical decision-making to achieve improved outcomes and reduced cost of care. This role owns the Evicore Medical Benefit Services (MBM) data science function responsible for predictive modeling, AI/ML and large language model (LLM) development, workflow automation, and analytical infrastructure connecting clinical data to operational outcomes.
[...]
Working in close collaboration with clinical, operational, product, and technology leadership, the leader will define and execute the organization’s AI strategy, including production AI pipelines, real-time decision support tools, and scalable automation solutions.
[...]
The role will lead the full lifecycle of advanced analytics—from research and validation through deployment, monitoring, and governance—while ensuring alignment with regulatory requirements and clinical best practices. The leader will also build and mentor a high-performing data science organization and drive innovation in areas such as intelligent automation and AI-assisted prior authorization.
[...]
- 8+ years of industry experience in data science or advanced analytics
- 5+ years in a leadership role managing teams and delivering production AI solutions
- Demonstrated experience deploying machine learning and/or LLM solutions in complex, regulated environments
[...]
- Deep expertise in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing, and Generative AI techniques
- Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Databricks) and modern data engineering practices
- Strong programming experience (Linux, Python, SQL, JavaScript) and familiarity with distributed systems and big data frameworks (e.g., Spark, Hadoop)
- Experience building scalable data pipelines, AI workflows, and production systems
- Healthcare data, clinical systems, or utilization management experience strongly preferred
[...]
- Comprehensive range of health-related benefits including medical, vision, dental, and well-being and behavioral health programs
- 401(k) and company paid life insurance
- Tuition reimbursement
- A minimum of 18 days of paid time off per year and paid holidays
- Annual salary of 178,500 - 297,500 USD / yearly, depending on relevant factors
- Eligibility to participate in an annual bonus and long term incentive plan
[...]

results.title: Data Science Senior Advisors- Hybrid | Jobflarely
highlights: position is Data Science Senior Advisor, which is a Band 4 Senior Contributor Career Track Role with Cigna-Evernorth Services Inc. Responsibilities- Act as subject matter expert in MLOps frameworks, bridging the gap between data science and engineering, ensuring models are developed, deployed, monitored and maintained effectively. Develop, productionalize, and support enterprise initiatives projects (e.g., deep learning foundational models). Develop, test, document, productionalize, and champion enterprise model lifecycle assets that enable consistency, reproducibility, and model governance for multiple domains (e.g., Clinical, UW/pricing, etc.) across multiple lines of business (e.g., US Medical, Pharmacy, and Benefits Management). Foster productive collaboration with engineering and infrastructure colleagues to optimize and enhance model lifecycle management and enable rapid value delivery. Develop and maintain strong collaborative relationships with technical and business matrix partners. Develop actionable value- and data-driven communication for internal and public-facing stakeholders to scale. Independently deliver clear and well-developed presentations for both technical and business audiences. Mentor Data Scientists and Data Engineering partners, both stateside and offshore, in all the responsibilities above. Hybrid work schedule. The salary for this position is $152,000.00 per year and this role is eligible for standard company benefits. Qualifications- Requires a Master’s degree in Data Science, Geographic Information Science, or a related quantitative field and 2 years of data science experience. Must have experience with: Python; SQL; R; Git; Machine Learning; Deep Learning; Statistical Analysis; Database Management; Natural Language Processing; Application Development; GitHub; PySpark; Databricks; PyTorch; and TensorFlow. If you will be working at home occasionally or permanently, the internet connection must be obtained through a cable broadband or fiber optic internet service provider with speeds of at least 10Mbps download/5Mbps upload. For this position, we anticipate offering an annual salary of 131,200 - 218,600 USD / yearly, depending on relevant factors, including experience and geographic location. This role is also anticipated to be eligible to participate in an annual bonus plan. At The Cigna Group, you’ll enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits, with a focus on supporting your whole health. Starting on day one of your employment, you’ll be offered several health-related benefits including medical, vision, dental, and well-being and behavioral health programs. We also offer 401(k), company paid life insurance, tuition reimbursement, a minimum of 18 days of paid time off per year and paid holidays. For more details on our employee benefits programs, click here. About The Cigna Group Doing something meaningful starts with a simple decision, a commitment to changing lives. At The Cigna Group, we’re dedicated to improving the health and vitality of those we serve. Through our divisions Cigna Healthcare and Evernorth Health Services, we are committed to enhancing the lives of our clients, customers and patients. Join us in driving growth and improving lives. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, age, disability, sex, childbirth (including pregnancy) or related medical conditions including but not limited to lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran or military status, religion, national origin, ancestry, marital or familial status, genetic information, status with regard to public assistance, citizenship status or any other characteristic protected by applicable equal employment opportunity laws. results.title: Senior Data Scientist (Service Ops Customer and Digital) - Work at Home | Hirevector
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist (Service Ops Customer and Digital) - Work at Home | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Senior Data Scientist (Service Ops Customer and Digital) - Work at Home

Posted May 28, 2026

### Summary

Our team leverages advanced Data Science (DS), augmented intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to help better predict our customers’ health outcomes, advance affordability, quality and accessibility of healthcare. We are recognized as a leader in leveraging Data & Analytics capabilities to create value for customers and stakeholders, enabling better decision making and health outcomes. This allows us to harness insights from the millions of lives we serve, helping our patient advocates and clinicians service our customers, ensuring they receive the best care from the highest performing medical providers. The speed in which DS, AI and ML are advancing means we are continuously learning and evolving our advanced data science capabilities.

As a Sr. Data Scientist, you will be working on both structured and unstructured data to build ML applications to support various healthcare-related business use cases that will improve our health system. Your work will require deep acquaintance with Evernorth’s data, seeking to find ways to deliver value through advanced feature engineering. From your models, you will deliver actionable insights that can be incorporated into existing Cigna wellness products as well as new programs. In addition to collaborating with Data Science Management to establish strategic priorities, you will work closely with Data & Engineering to deliver code that is robust and ready to scale.

The ideal candidate for this role will have strong coding skills (preferably in Python, PySpark), strong knowledge about ML algorithms and NLP techniques, a passion for creating technology to improve the healthcare systems, and an attitude of creativity and continual learning.

- Responsibilities
- Create models using NLP, ML / AI techniques to predict risk/cost and identify actionable insights
- Deliver clean, reusable, and scalable code
- Work closely with Data & Engineering to deploy models
- Provide thought leadership on the latest analytic techniques, technologies, and methods

- Qualifications
- 5+ years' of industry experience related to Data Mining, Statistical Analysis, ML Algorithms, Gen-AI and NLP Techniques
- Strong experience with Python and its data science libraries such as Scikit-learn, Pandas, Numpy, and Keras
- Strong understanding of NLP techniques such as information extraction, sentiment analysis, text summarization, and topic modeling
- Strong understanding of ML/AI methods such as SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, Light GBM, Deep Learning (CNN, RNN, LSTM), Reinforcement Learning, Optimization
- Strong programming experience including understanding of concepts in data structures, algorithms, and software engineering principles
- Good understanding and experience with traditional data science approach like sampling techniques, feature engineering, classification, and regression.
- Have experience in building forecasting model for trend analysis
- Excellent oral and written communication skills
- Prior experience working in the healthcare industry is highly desirable
- Experience with deploying advanced natural language models and time-series model into production is highly desirable
- MS/PhD in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, or in any technical field that provides a solid basis for analytics or equivalent work experience

Apply tot his job

Apply for This Job

results.title: AI/ML Engineer /Data Scientist with AI/ML :: Hybrid (Morris Plains NJ, Bloomfield CT, Austin, or Dallas TX office) | Corp to Corp
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer /Data Scientist with AI/ML :: Hybrid (Morris Plains NJ, Bloomfield CT, Austin, or Dallas TX office) | Corp to Corp Get all C2C Jobs / hotlists 🔥 Alerts

US jobs, C2C Hotlists, vendor list and Staffing News

Role: AI/ML Engineer /Data Scientist with AI/ML Work Type: Hybrid (Morris Plains NJ, Bloomfield CT, Austin, or Dallas TX office)

Client: Cigna (F2F interview) YOE:10+

Agentic AI experience required. This is most important skill.

Primary Responsibilities:

- Lead the development and training of machine learning models.
- Optimize AI algorithms for performance, efficiency, and scalability.
- Design and implement robust and scalable data pipelines.
- Integrate AI systems with enterprise software and cloud-based applications.
- Drive data-driven decision-making by working with cross-functional teams.
- Plan and lead analytics projects, ensuring alignment with business goals.
- Present findings and insights to stakeholders, translating complex technical results into actionable business recommendations.
- Ensure quality and compliance of data and modeling results with business and regulatory requirements.
- Mentor and guide junior data scientists, fostering technical growth and best practices in AI/ML development.

Required Qualifications & Skills:

- At least 6 years of experience developing AI and ML solutions in a professional setting.
- Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Data Science, or a related field (Master's preferred).
- Experience developing AI-driven chatbots using RAG and AI agents with LangChain.
- Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL for data science applications.
- Strong understanding of machine learning techniques, including supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.
- Expertise in deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers) and NLP techniques.
- Proficiency in designing scalable and maintainable AI systems, including microservices and REST API development.
- Hands-on experience with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, Databricks, Azure, and GCP.
- Knowledge of MLOps best practices, including CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and role-based access provisioning.
- Strong ability to communicate AI/ML insights and model performance to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications:

- Expertise in mathematical concepts, including statistics, probability, linear algebra, and calculus.
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks such as Apache Spark.
- Familiarity with advanced AI tools and platforms, including PyTorch, Hugging Face, and Kafka.
- Strong project management and leadership skills, with experience driving AI/ML initiatives in a business environment.
- System design expertise, ensuring scalability and robustness in AI deployments.

Thanks & Regards,

Ravikar Yadav

Technical Recruiter

Concept Software and Services Inc.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer – Express Scripts Canada, Mississauga, ON | Joblyz
highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer – Express Scripts Canada, Mississauga, ON | Joblyz

Express Scripts Canada, part of The Cigna Group, is hiring a Machine Learning Engineer in Mississauga, Ontario. This hybrid full-time role supports data science, machine learning development, AI solutions, and advanced analytics within a healthcare services environment.

Sponsored Links

## ABOUT THE JOB

The Machine Learning Engineer position focuses on building and maintaining machine learning models, data pipelines, and AI-driven solutions that support healthcare operations and business decision-making. The successful candidate will work with data scientists, engineers, technology teams, and business stakeholders to develop scalable solutions, improve model performance, and support data-driven innovation.

Sponsored Links

## KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

- Design, develop, and maintain machine learning models and data solutions.
- Build and optimize data pipelines for analytics and AI applications.
- Collaborate with data science, engineering, and business teams on technical projects.
- Support model deployment, monitoring, testing, and performance improvement.
- Work with structured and unstructured healthcare and business data.
- Maintain documentation, code quality, and technical standards.
- Follow data privacy, security, and compliance requirements in all work.

## WHY WORK HERE?

Working with Express Scripts Canada and The Cigna Group may include competitive compensation, hybrid work opportunities, professional development programs, collaborative technology teams, wellness resources, and employee support programs depending on eligibility.

## HOW TO APPLY FOR THIS POSITION

Interested candidates can apply directly through the official The Cigna Group careers website using the button above.

## SHARE THIS JOB

highlights:
Senior Data Scientist (Service Ops Customer and Digital) - Work at Home | Quickswoop

Back to Job Board

# Senior Data Scientist (Service Ops Customer and Digital) - Work at Home

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-06-06

Full-time

Summary

Our team leverages advanced Data Science (DS), augmented intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to help better predict our customers’ health outcomes, advance affordability, quality and accessibility of healthcare. We are recognized as a leader in leveraging Data & Analytics capabilities to create value for customers and stakeholders, enabling better decision making and health outcomes. This allows us to harness insights from the millions of lives we serve, helping our patient advocates and clinicians service our customers, ensuring they receive the best care from the highest performing medical providers. The speed in which DS, AI and ML are advancing means we are continuously learning and evolving our advanced data science capabilities.

As a Sr. Data Scientist, you will be working on both structured and unstructured data to build ML applications to support various healthcare-related business use cases that will improve our health system. Your work will require deep acquaintance with Evernorth’s data, seeking to find ways to deliver value through advanced feature engineering. From your models, you will deliver actionable insights that can be incorporated into existing Cigna wellness products as well as new programs. In addition to collaborating with Data Science Management to establish strategic priorities, you will work closely with Data & Engineering to deliver code that is robust and ready to scale.

The ideal candidate for this role will have strong coding skills (preferably in Python, PySpark), strong knowledge about ML algorithms and NLP techniques, a passion for creating technology to improve the healthcare systems, and an attitude of creativity and continual learning.

- Responsibilities

- Qualifications

Data Analyst for our Pre/PostPay Edit editing program. This position will provide the technical component of our Pre/PostPay Edit editing program in development, implementation, production monitoring and maintenance of our internal PrePay edits. Strong technical coding experience will be needed to be successful in this role.

process testing and implementation.

results.title: Generative AI Scientist - (Model Risk & Validation) | Wfh
highlights:
Generative AI Scientist - (Model Risk & Validation) | Wfh
[...]
# Generative AI Scientist - (Model Risk & Validation)
[...]
Join a recently formed team focused on Model Risk and Responsible AI. The Generative AI Scientist - Risk will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills to reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. As a Data Scientist on this team, you will focus on three main project areas: Model Validation, Model Metrics and Monitoring, and Responsible AI. This requires someone with depth in AI/ML/GenAI from a data science perspective, versatility to think in terms of technology systems, and some understanding of emerging areas of Responsible AI and AI Ethics. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
[...]
- As a Generative AI Scientist within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects.
[...]
- Conduct independent model validation of existing models for benchmarking, assessment, and gauging effectiveness.
[...]
- Determine aspects of model drift and related data drift for the purpose of model risk management (MRM) to both reduce risk and also find opportunities to drive new revenue growth and innovation. Apply deep expertise with AI/ML/GenAI model development, including hands-on experience with model building and model evaluation.
[...]
- Benchmark and potentially rebuild existing models as needed using updated data, and potentially newer, more modern and effective algorithms.
[...]
- Actively drive improvements in model monitoring activities, including methods for model registration, model metadata management, and conceptualizing approaches for related tools and techniques. Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting.
[...]
- Graduate Degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.
- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
[...]
- 1+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
[...]
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
[...]
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
[...]
- Preferred Qualifications:
[...]
- Familiarity with healthcare payor ecosystem and related data.
- General understanding of Responsible AI (RAI), including explainability (XAI), AI NIST RMF, and related AI risk management frameworks.
[...]
- Experience and understanding evaluating models for bias and fairness, with aptitude for detecting bias in the model design and data, as well as using metrics such as SHAP and LIME.
[...]
- Understanding appropriate model metrics and techniques for managing, evaluating and monitoring GenAI models and LLMs
[...]
- Understanding and familiarity with model governance and data governance best practices.
[...]
- Strong understanding of technology systems for model development (e.g., Python, DataRobot, AWS Sagemaker), model deployments (AWS, Azure, DataRobot, DataBricks), model monitoring (AWS Model Monitor, MLFlow, NannyML, FiddlerAI, Arize) and related tools for model management and metadata management.

# Consulting Manager – AI Engineer – Insurance & Retirement
[...]
As an Consulting Manager – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technical Architect – Insurance & Retirement , you will make an impact by designing and guiding the delivery of secure, scalable AI-powered applications that solve complex business problems for insurance and retirement clients . You will be a valued member of the Insurance & Retirement Consulting team and work collaboratively with product leaders, data and engineering teams, enterprise architects, and client stakeholders to move AI solutions from concept to production.
[...]
- Lead end-to-end AI solution architecture for custom applications-creating solution blueprints, reference architectures, and detailed designs that connect models, data pipelines, application layers, and integrations.
[...]
- Define AI platform and model strategy by selecting appropriate approaches (machine learning, generative AI, large language models), orchestration patterns, inference strategies, and lifecycle management that meet enterprise standards.
[...]
- Architect data and integration patterns for AI use cases, including ingestion, feature engineering, training pipelines, and real-time/batch inference-integrated with core systems through APIs and event-driven approaches.
[...]
What you must have to be considered
[...]
- 7-10 years in software engineering and/or architecture, with strong experience designing cloud-native applications (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform), microservices, APIs, and modern DevOps practices.
[...]
- Hands-on experience designing and implementing AI solutions , including machine learning and generative AI/large language model solutions, with practical model orchestration patterns.
[...]
- Experience designing machine learning operations (MLOps) approaches for model training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement in production environments.
[...]

[...]

[...]
- Exposure to emerging enterprise AI patterns such as agentic AI , AI copilots , and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) .
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
[...]
- Strong familiarity with enterprise security, privacy, and governance expectations for AI solutions.

- Track record of building reusable accelerators, best practices, and reference architectures that improve delivery speed and quality.
